details: Found two tanhshrink compute kernels: tanhshrink_kernel.cpp (FPU-based binary sub) and tanhshrink_sfpu_kernel.cpp (SFPU-based binary sub). Both use tanh_tile(). Need to determine which one is actually used.
_llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_params_ - VectorMode::RC default, 4 face loop with ITERATIONS=8 per face, SETRWC between faces

TANHSHRINK has a dedicated compute kernel (tanhshrink_kernel.cpp) that does NOT use SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0 macro. Instead it explicitly calls tanh_tile() then uses binary_dest_reuse_tiles for subtraction. The second kernel (tanhshrink_sfpu_kernel.cpp) uses sub_binary_tile SFPU approach. Both exist in the codebase but TANHSHRINK cannot go through get_op_init_and_func_default as it would TT_THROW. Need to determine if the UnaryProgramFactory is even used or if there is a custom program factory.

HARDTANH UnaryOpType exists in enum, is_parametrized_type returns true. But no case in get_op_init_and_func_parameterized or get_op_init_and_func_default. No hardtanh_tile API function exists. Core SFPU kernel _calculate_hardtanh_ exists in tt_llk for wormhole_b0 and blackhole.

Complete dispatch chain is missing. No hardtanh_tile API, no LLK dispatch, no case in get_op_init_and_func. Core SFPU kernel _calculate_hardtanh_ exists. ADDR_MOD_7 used (default for hardtanh since no special addr_mod case). Wormhole uses SETRWC for face progression, Blackhole uses inc_dst_addr.

CRITICAL FINDING: The tanh SFPU kernel (llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh) is MISSING from the codebase. It is called in compute_kernel_api.h but never defined. The tanhshrink compute kernels (tanhshrink_kernel.cpp, tanhshrink_sfpu_kernel.cpp) exist but cannot compile because tanh_tile() calls this missing function. Additionally, TANHSHRINK is not in get_op_init_and_func_default so it would TT_THROW at dispatch time. The operation is currently BROKEN/UNIMPLEMENTED.

key_findings: Uses sfpi::abs, sfpi::vFloat arithmetic, sfpi::dst_reg, v_if/v_endif for piecewise sigmoid approximation then multiplied by x. Polynomial+linear+saturation sigmoid, 3 v_if branches.

details: HARDTANH dispatch chain is INCOMPLETE: UnaryOpType::HARDTANH exists in enum, is_parametrized_type returns true, but no case in get_op_init_and_func, no hardtanh_tile compute API, no LLK dispatch. Core SFPU kernel _calculate_hardtanh_ exists in tt_llk. Operation would fail at runtime due to TT_THROW in get_op_init_and_func_parameterized default case.

key_findings: Identical on WH and BH. Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, dst_reg, v_if/v_endif). Implements hardtanh via subtraction-clamp-add trick with 3 pre-encoded FP16_B parameters. No APPROXIMATION_MODE dependency.

cc_pattern: v_if/v_endif predicated blocks for piecewise sigmoid

details: TANHSHRINK has dedicated compute kernels (tanhshrink_kernel.cpp, tanhshrink_sfpu_kernel.cpp) that do NOT use SFPU_OP_CHAIN_0. The operation is currently BROKEN: (1) get_op_init_and_func_default has no TANHSHRINK case -> TT_THROW, (2) tanh_tile calls llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh which has no definition in the codebase (SFPU tanh kernel removed), (3) calculate_sfpu_binary wrapper referenced but never defined.

key_findings: SUB branch: result = in0 - in1 via SFPMAD. tanh SFPU kernel is MISSING. Binary subtraction uses _calculate_sfpu_binary_ with BinaryOp::SUB. ADDR_MOD_7 with dest.incr=0.

instructions_decoded: SFPMAD (via vFloat subtraction: result = in0 - in1)
